steps.title: Spot the agreement signal
steps.content: The word "concurring" is the key signal—it indicates that Keller is supporting the same conclusion about glass availability that Anton reached.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition/similarity — Anton reached a conclusion about glass availability, and Keller concurred with the same conclusion. The blank needs a transition signaling parallel agreement.
  • Choice A — As such,: signals a consequence or conclusion drawn from what preceded it, so fails — Keller's concurrence is not caused by Anton's finding. ✗
  • Choice B — Likewise,: signals that a second party does or concludes the same thing, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — Nonetheless,: signals contrast (despite X, Y still holds), so fails — Keller agrees with Anton rather than opposing him. ✗
  • Choice D — Consequently,: signals a direct result of what preceded it, so fails — Keller's agreement is parallel, not a consequence of Anton's work. ✗
That matches Choice B — Likewise,.
